hero.title: Reshaping Healthcare in Asia-Pacific with Open Standards
hero.subtitle: The APAC Precision Health Initiative drives SMART on FHIR adoption across the region — enabling secure health data interoperability and precision medicine innovation.

metrics.title: The Growth Opportunity in APAC Digital Health
metrics.subtitle: Asia-Pacific is the fastest-growing region for SMART on FHIR adoption worldwide

smartIntro.title: What is SMART on FHIR?
smartIntro.description: SMART on FHIR is an open-standards platform developed by Boston Children's Hospital's Computational Health Informatics Program (CHIP). It enables third-party apps to securely read and write electronic health records — like an app store for healthcare, where any developer can build medical applications that run on any FHIR-enabled system.
smartIntro.link: Learn more about SMART on FHIR →

apacPreview.title: Why Asia-Pacific?
apacPreview.subtitle: Home to 60% of the world's population, Asia-Pacific faces critical challenges — fragmented health data, aging populations, and rising costs. SMART on FHIR offers a unified, open solution.
apacPreview.pain1Desc: 90% of APAC healthcare facilities use incompatible systems. Data silos severely hinder collaborative care.
apacPreview.pain2Desc: By 2050, APAC's 65+ population will double. Chronic disease management demands interconnected digital infrastructure.
apacPreview.pain3Desc: Standardised interoperability can save each hospital millions annually in integration and maintenance costs.
